channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A cancer vaccine made just for you. mRNA is back and it's fighting melanoma
item.description: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.content:encoded: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo near 300 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Is bovine colostrum really 'liquid gold' for gut health?
item.description: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.content:encoded: Baby calves rely on it to build up their immune systems and gut. And now marketers are promoting it for humans. Here's what scientists say.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: How aid cuts are hampering the front-line response to the Ebola crisis
item.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching an Ebola crisis unfold in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.
item.content:encoded:
Aid workers in Uganda are watching an Ebola crisis unfold in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

(Image credit: Leonard Musinguzi)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
item.description: A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.
item.content:encoded:
A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

(Image credit: Min Yu/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The biggest permanent desert lake threatens with rising waters and hungry crocs
item.description: Kenya's Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake. Its waters have long sustained hundreds of thousands. Now the lake is facing multiple threats — and threatening those who rely on it.
item.content:encoded:
Kenya's Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake. Its waters have long sustained hundreds of thousands. Now the lake is facing multiple threats — and threatening those who rely on it.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Text and photos by Tommy Trenchard

item.title: Amid a slew of chemical accidents, the agency that investigates them is under threat
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with former member of the U.S. Chemical Safety Board Rick Engler about the current state of chemical safety following a string of incidents in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks with former member of the U.S. Chemical Safety Board Rick Engler about the current state of chemical safety following a string of incidents in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Pancreatic cancer was once considered almost untreatable. A new drug is offering hope
item.description: A new drug for pancreatic cancer gives some hope for one of the most dire types of cancer.
item.content:encoded: A new drug for pancreatic cancer gives some hope for one of the most dire types of cancer.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Common myths about gut health
item.description: Marketers sell all kinds of tests and supplements to fix your gut health. But is any of this actually worth spending money on?
item.content:encoded: Marketers sell all kinds of tests and supplements to fix your gut health. But is any of this actually worth spending money on?
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Why are people attacking Ebola clinics? It revolves around trust, death and body bags
item.description: In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.
item.content:encoded:
In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.

(Image credit: Gradel Muyisa Mumbere)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel
